Executive Control Center
Synthesis view — twin S-curve, EVM, watch list, heat strip · Phase 2 deliverable
Phase 1 — Skeleton in place

What this module will become

The Executive Control Center is the flagship synthesis view — the single screen a CE / DG / PD opens first. All numbers compute on the fly from authoritative state; nothing is duplicated.

  • Hero strip — six headline KPIs (Contract Value, Physical %, Financial %, CPI, SPI, Days)
  • Twin S-Curve overlay — physical baseline, physical actual, financial baseline, financial actual, divergence band
  • EVM snapshot card — PV / EV / AC bars with CV, SV, EAC, VAC, TCPI
  • Cash Flow horizon — augmented with look-ahead-derived forward pipeline
  • Critical watch list — IPC aging breaches, retention release, DLP forecast, behind-plan WBS, materials below ROL, plant breakdown, mapping coverage, claim-vs-progress divergence
  • Reconciliation heat strip — physical-vs-financial per Bill (1–12) and per WBS Level-1
  • Quick actions — + New IPC · + New RAR · Update %-Complete · Open Mapping · Backup · Print Executive Brief
Executive view · —
Phase 5 · Watch List

Critical Items Requiring Attention

Auto-curated alerts across all integration touchpoints. Highest-severity items surfaced first. Each item links its source data so the PD/CE can drill down.

Phase 5 · Heat Strip

Per-Bill Health · 4-Axis Heat Map

Each of the 12 Bills rated on Mapping Coverage, SPI (schedule), Unbilled Physical %, and Activity health. Red cells flag bills needing intervention.

Touchpoint A · Live

Twin S-Curve · Financial vs Physical

IPC values (Commercial) aligned to monthly buckets via Period→Month mapping, overlaid against physical baseline and actual progress (Execution). Divergence = financial running ahead of or behind physical reality.

Cumulative Curves · 37-month baseline · Dual axes (Physical % left, PKR M right)
Touchpoint B · Live

EVM Snapshot · Earned Value Management

Proper scope-side EVM: each BOQ item's value is earned by the weighted-average actual progress of its mapped WBS activities. BAC = contract value; AC = resource cost (Store + Plant + Eq). Unmapped BOQ items contribute to BAC but not EV — see the per-Bill breakdown for diagnostics.

PV · EV · AC at cutoff (PKR M)
Per-Bill Breakdown · BOQ value × WBS actual %
Touchpoint C · Live

Cash Flow Forecast · 6-Month Forward Pipeline

Three streams forecast forward from today: (1) in-flight IPCs predicted to pay based on aging thresholds, (2) forward-billable value from Look-Ahead activities completing in the next 60 days, (3) in-flight RAR disbursements to S/C contractors. Net position projected month-by-month.

Monthly Cash Flow (PKR M) · In · Out · Cumulative Net
Month-by-Month Forecast
Touchpoint D · Live

Secure Advance Recovery · Consumption-Derived

Each secure advance is compared against the value of material physically consumed via BOQ executed quantities × BOQ↔Material consumption factors. When implied consumption exceeds outstanding balance, the system flags that the advance is fully "absorbed" by completed work — and Phase 4-D auto-fills proportional recovery proposals at IPC vetting.

Active Secure Advances · Outstanding vs Implied Consumption
Touchpoint E · Live

Unbilled Physical Progress · Ready to Bill

Walks every BOQ via BOQ↔WBS mapping, compares weighted actual % against the cumulative quantity already claimed in IPCs, and surfaces the rupee value of work that is physically complete but not yet on any IPC. Open a new IPC and click ⚡ Suggest from Physical Progress to pre-fill all quantities.

Top 12 BoQ items with unbilled physical value
Touchpoint F · Live

Governance · Unified Audit Log & RBAC Matrix

Every state mutation across Commercial, Execution, and Mapping is recorded. This card exposes the full audit timeline (filterable + exportable) and the role-based permission matrix that governs who can perform each action.

Filter: —
▶ Role-Based Access Control Matrix click to expand

Why a synthesis view matters

Today, the QS opens the Commercial app to draft an IPC, the Planner opens the NLC dashboard to update progress, and there is no shared view. A divergence between claim and physical progress can persist for weeks before anyone notices. The Executive Control Center exists so the gap between physical and financial reality cannot hide. Phase 4-A delivers the twin S-curve above; subsequent phases will add the EVM card, cash-flow forecast, critical watch list, and per-Bill heat strip.

IPC Register (Client Billing)
All Interim Payment Certificates submitted to Consultant · 7-stage pipeline (draft → submitted → vetted → forwarded → approved → ack → paid)

Bill subcontractors and labour contractors for work executed under their CA. Contractor-specific deductions apply.
RAR Setup 0 items · PKR 0
RAR Deductions
RAR Net Payable to Contractor
PKR 0
Gross PKR 0 · Deductions PKR 0
RAR Register (Subcontractor & Labour Billing)
All Running Account Receipts issued · 6-stage pipeline (draft → validated → verified → approved → marked → paid)
Filter —
Advances Ledger
Two-sided ledgers for Mobilisation (against contract) and Secure (against material procurement) advances. Recovery happens through IPCs and RARs.
Escalation
Price-adjustment payments. Two distinct paths: formula-based EPCs for the main contract (FGEHA → NLC), and negotiated sublet escalation for S/C contractors (handled inline in RAR Validate/Approve).
Formula-based Escalation Payment Certificates · Phase 2E-6
One EPC per IPC. Price adjustment coefficient Pn derived from PBS index movements weighted by contractual components. Amount = IPC_gross × (Pn − 1). Approval pipeline: draft → submitted → vetted → approved → paid.
PBS Index Master · Base & Current Values
Weights must sum to 1.000. Edit current indices to drive Pn.
EPC Pipeline
Retention
Cumulative retention deducted across all IPCs. DLP (Defects Liability Period) split happens when Final Bill is client-approved.
Reconciliation
Compare what NLC bills FGEHA (IPCs) against what NLC pays sub-contractors (RARs). Per-IPC and per-Contractor coverage views with linkage editor.
Cash Flow
6-month rolling treasury view. Inflows from in-flight IPCs + forward-billable value; outflows from in-flight RARs. EPCs & DLP release will be added in Phase 2E-6.
Monthly Cash Flow Projection (PKR M)
Month-by-Month Cash Flow Detail
Methodology: IPC cash-in dates predicted by adding remaining aging-threshold days to the document\'s current stage entry timestamp. Forward-billable value derived from Look-Ahead activities × BOQ↔WBS mapping × unbilled portion. RAR cash-out dates predicted similarly via RAR pipeline aging. EPC pipeline (Phase 2E-6), Final Bill / DLP release (Phase 2F), and sub-contractor mob/secure advance recovery (Phase 4-D) are pending integration into this forecast.
Aging
Every in-pipeline document (IPC · RAR · EPC) with days-in-stage tracking and breach detection. Critical urgency triggers when documents exceed 2× their stage threshold.
Group by

Revenue (executed) vs. costs (sublet/labour incurred), gross margin, and per-contractor scorecard.

Resource Curve · Cost Forecast (Earned-Value Style)
BAC = plan-to-complete · AC = actual cost incurred · EV = earned value at data date · CPI = EV/AC · EAC = AC + (BAC − EV) / CPI
Cost Buildup · Planned · Actual (per stream) · Forecast to EAC

Executive Progress Report
Generated — · Data date —
WBS Roll-up at Data Date
Delayed Activities Requiring Attention
Active Work Fronts
Mapping
The bridge between Commercial (BOQ) and Execution (WBS). Hybrid auto-suggest with manual confirm. Powers EVM, reconciliation, and the integration touchpoints.
BOQ Items
Status: —
Select a BOQ item to map →
Links each BOQ item to one or more STORE_DATA materials with consumption factors per 1 BoQ unit. Example: 1 cum RCC consumes ~7 bags cement + ~0.4 t aggregate + ~110 kg steel. The factor multiplies BoQ executed quantity to derive implied material consumption — used for material reconciliation and the secure-advance auto-recovery (Phase 4-D).
After auto-suggest, the QS must enter the consumption factor (per BoQ unit) for each linked material.
BOQ Items
Status: —
Select a BOQ item →
Bridges free-text IPC and RAR period labels ("Oct 2025", "Q4-25", "First Half 2026", "Apr-Jun 2026") to NLC monthly buckets ("Apr-26", "Apr-26/May-26/Jun-26"). The auto-parser recognises 8 patterns. Without this mapping, financial S-curve cannot be aligned to physical S-curve in Phase 4-A.
Auto-parsed entries land as "suggested" — confirm or edit each.
Doc: Status: —

Portfolio
Cross-project KPI rollup across all PD HQs · read-only · click a row to open that project
—
Command Center
Hierarchical roll-up · HQ NLC → HQ Engrs → PD HQs → projects · click to drill down
—
RAG Risk Thresholds
Tune the amber/red cut-offs for collection, receivables and schedule slippage. Changes recolour every dashboard, breadcrumb and brief instantly.
—
Project Details
Set a project’s client, consultant, contract reference and start / finish dates. These appear in the header and on every page when that project is selected.
—
Demo Data
Populate the system with hypothetical projects across every PD HQ to explore the command hierarchy, portfolio and roll-ups end to end.
—
Import Baseline Plans
Load a project’s planned progress S-curve and/or schedule (XLSX / CSV / paste). Both become per-project and drive that project’s charts.
—
Add Project & Import BOQ
Create a project under a PD HQ and load its Bill of Quantities (XLSX / CSV / paste). Contract value is derived from the line items.
—
Settings & Diagnostics
Storage status, theme, audit log, backup & restore
